pathways: Mitochondrial energy | Axon regeneration | NGF signaling
metrics: VAS pain -38% vs placebo; nerve fiber density +29% on biopsy (ALADIN/SYDNEY)
study_model: 1257 patients across 2 RCTs; sural nerve biopsy
notes: Best evidence for physical nerve fiber regrowth of any oral supplement

metrics: TSS neuropathy score -51% at 5 weeks (SYDNEY 2); NNT ~2.7 for 50% pain reduction
dose: 600 mg/day IV or oral for 3–5 weeks; oral 600–1800 mg/day maintenance
notes: IV route faster onset; oral maintenance valid for chronic use

pathways: Myelin synthesis | One-carbon metabolism | Schwann cell function
metrics: VAS -42% vs cyanocobalamin; remyelination confirmed on nerve conduction studies
dose: 1500–3000 mcg/day oral (methylcobalamin form only); 500 mcg IM 3×/week for acute
study_model: Human RCT; nerve conduction velocity studies
source: Guida et al.; Okada et al.
notes: Methylcobalamin only — cyanocobalamin does NOT cross blood-nerve barrier

pathways: VDR-myelin gene activation | NGF upregulation | Nrf2
metrics: Myelin-associated gene activation within 24h; axon diameter increase in nerve injury models
source: Garcion et al.; multiple spinal pain RCTs
notes: Acts as steroid hormone on VDR receptors in Schwann cells; deficiency extremely common in chronic back pain

pathways: NGF synthesis | TrkA signaling | Axon elongation
metrics: Cognitive/nerve function RCT improvements; hericenones cross BBB and stimulate NGF synthesis
study_model: Human RCT (cognition/nerve); in vitro NGF induction
source: Mori et al.; Lai et al.
notes: Only oral compound with direct NGF stimulation activity

pathways: Axon reconstruction | Synapse regeneration | NF-κB suppression
metrics: Axon regrowth and synapse reconstruction in damaged neurons (Br J Pharmacol)
study_model: Animal model (Bhattacharya et al.); human stress/cortisol RCTs
source: British Journal of Pharmacology; KSM-66 human trials
notes: Withanolide A specifically — not all ashwagandha extracts standardized to this compound

metrics: VAS -56% in sciatica RCT (Guida et al.); meta-analysis NNT 3.1 for 50% pain reduction
source: Guida et al.; Paladini et al. meta-analysis 2019
notes: Use ultramicronized (um-PEA) for best bioavailability; very safe, no known drug interactions

cytokines: TNF-α | IL-1β | IL-6 | PGE2 (all resolve, not just suppress)
pathways: Resolvin/protectin pathway | SPM receptor (ALX/FPR2) | Neuroinflammation resolution | Macrophage phenotype switching (M1→M2)
metrics: Chronic pain RCT: pain scores -38% vs standard omega-3; inflammatory resolution confirmed via lipid mediator profiling; distinct from EPA/DHA suppression effect
dose: 2 softgels/day (1g fractionated marine lipid concentrate standardized to 18-HEPE, 17-HDHA, 14-HDHA); Metagenics SPM Active or equivalent
study_model: Human RCT (chronic pain); in vitro neuroinflammation resolution models
source: Metagenics SPM Active clinical data; Serhan et al. resolvin/protectin literature; Headland & Norling 2015
notes: NOT the same as standard fish oil — these are downstream SPM metabolites that actively resolve inflammation rather than passively suppress it. Switches macrophages from pro-inflammatory (M1) to pro-resolution (M2) phenotype. Professional-grade brand physicians recognize. US Patents 11,077,083 and 11,077,084.

pathways: NMDA receptor antagonism | Calcium channel blocking | Central sensitization reversal
metrics: IV magnesium reduced central sensitization pain in RCTs; oral reduces hyperalgesia
dose: 400–600 mg elemental magnesium/day as glycinate (best absorption, least GI effect)
source: Crosby et al.; Pickering et al.
notes: Critical for patients with central sensitization component; most are deficient

pathways: NF-κB | Nrf2 | IVD chondrocyte protection | ADAMTS inhibition
metrics: Blocked IL-1β-induced IVD cell death; reduced MMP-13 by 67% in nucleus pulposus cells
dose: 500–1000 mg/day (use hesperidin methylchalcone or phytosome for bioavailability)
study_model: In vitro (IVD nucleus pulposus cells); animal disc models
source: PMC hesperidin IVD studies; Fisetin/flavonoid IVD literature
notes: Most relevant for disc degeneration component; pairs well with Boswellia for IVD protection

metrics: Dendritic branching increase 30–45% in hippocampal neurons; neuroprotective in ischemia models
dose: 300–450 mg/day standardized to 20% bacosides (take with fat)
notes: Take with fat; may cause mild GI upset initially

metrics: VAS -58% vs placebo in spinal OA; Oswestry Disability Index -14 points in DDD RCT
dose: 100–250 mg AKBA (5-Loxin or ApresFlex) standardized extract; take with fat
source: Sengupta et al.; Chopra et al.
notes: Specifically need AKBA-standardized extract (5-Loxin/ApresFlex); generic Boswellia resin is less effective

metrics: CRP -35% vs placebo; VAS pain reduction comparable to ibuprofen in knee OA RCT
dose: 500–1000 mg curcumin phytosome or BCM-95 2×/day + 5–20 mg piperine
source: Kuptniratsaikul et al.; Panahi et al.
notes: Bioavailability is the critical variable — standard curcumin powder is nearly useless; use phytosome, BCM-95, or nanoparticle form

pathways: Resolvin/protectin synthesis | COX-2 competitive inhibition | NF-κB
metrics: Neck/back pain RCT: 59% reported NSAIDs unnecessary after 75 days; TNF-α -32%
source: Maroon & Bost 2006; ORIGIN trial; multiple meta-analyses
notes: Use triglyceride (not ethyl ester) form; take with largest meal; may thin blood at high doses. For active inflammation resolution upgrade to SPM Active (18-HEPE/17-HDHA/14-HDHA)

pathways: NF-κB | NLRP3 inflammasome | GABA-A modulation | Nrf2
metrics: Sciatic nerve injury model: allodynia -68%; IL-1β -54% in spinal dorsal horn
source: Zhang et al.; Shen et al.; Eclectic pharmacopoeia (nervous system sedative)
notes: Traditional use: nervous exhaustion, nerve inflammation (Eclectic physicians, 19th c.); NLRP3 data strongest of botanical tier

pathways: Adenosine A1 receptor | Schwann cell survival | NF-κB
metrics: Schwann cell apoptosis reduced 60%; neuropathic allodynia reduced in CCI model
dose: 1–3g dried root/day as decoction; 300–600 mg paeoniflorin extract
study_model: Animal (CCI neuropathic model); in vitro Schwann cell
source: Liu et al.; TCM pharmacopoeia; Eclectic use (muscle spasm, nerve pain)
notes: Traditional use: muscle spasm + nerve pain combo (TCM, Eclectic); Schwann cell protection pathway unique among botanicals
